name: Brivo Cloud Access Control Platform API
what_it_does: Brivo cloud-based commercial access control REST API for commercial real estate operators, enterprise security teams, and technology integrators to manage door access credentials, user groups, access schedules, video surveillance, and visitor management across Brivo's cloud-managed hardware — enabling centralized multi-site access control management and integration with HR, identity, and building automation systems. Enables AI agents to manage user credential for employee access provisioning automation, handle access group for role-based permission management automation, access door unlock for remote entry automation, retrieve access log for security audit automation, manage schedule for time-based access control automation, handle visitor management for guest access automation, access video integration for event-triggered recording automation, retrieve multi-site for property portfolio management automation, manage SCIM provisioning for HR identity sync automation, and integrate Brivo with HRIS, identity providers, and building automation for enterprise access control automation.
use_cases: Provisioning and deprovisioning employee access credentials through HRIS integration automation agents
Managing multi-site commercial real estate access control for property portfolio automation agents
Accessing door access logs and security events for compliance audit automation agents
Integrating Brivo with SCIM and SSO for enterprise identity lifecycle management automation agents
not_for: Residential smart lock (Brivo is commercial enterprise access control, not consumer home security)
High-security government facilities (Brivo is commercial/enterprise, not government-certified FICAM)
Offline-only access control without internet (Brivo requires cloud connectivity)
best_when: A commercial real estate operator, enterprise security team, or technology integrator wanting AI agents to manage multi-site access control, credential provisioning, and security events through Brivo's cloud-managed access control platform.
avoid_when: HARDWARE DEPENDENCY: Brivo API manages Brivo hardware (readers, panels, credentials) only; automated generic access control hardware assumption creates hardware incompatibility; automated must have Brivo hardware installed. OAUTH REQUIRED: Brivo API uses OAuth 2.0 with refresh tokens; automated single-call API key assumption creates auth token expiry; automated must implement token refresh flow for long-running integrations. ACCOUNT HIERARCHY: Brivo organizes sites, groups, and users in hierarchical account structure; automated flat permission model assumption creates incorrect permission assignment; automated must understand Brivo's account→site→group→user hierarchy. HARDWARE ENROLLMENT: New Brivo credentials (cards, mobile) must be physically enrolled before remote management; automated remote-only enrollment assumption creates missing physical activation step.

auth.notes: Brivo uses OAuth 2.0 with access and refresh tokens for API authentication. REST API with JSON. Bethesda, MD HQ. Founded 2001 by Ron Rezek and Dean Drako. Products: Cloud access control, video surveillance, visitor management, mobile app credentials, smart reader. SDKs: None public. SOC 2 Type II certified. GDPR, CCPA compliant. SCIM support. 25M+ users managed. 600,000+ doors secured. Enterprise commercial focus. Competes with Kisi and OpenPath for cloud access control.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Developer sandbox for approved integrators; production requires Brivo commercial agreement
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-door per-month subscription; enterprise pricing; hardware cost separate
pricing.notes: Bethesda MD. SOC 2 certified. Per-door subscription. Hardware required. Enterprise commercial focus.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth token; access and credential APIs throttled; webhooks for access events

known_agent_gotchas: TOKEN REFRESH REQUIRED: Brivo OAuth tokens expire; automated single token assumption creates 401 after expiry; automated must implement token refresh flow to maintain long-running API sessions

BRIVO HARDWARE REQUIRED: Brivo API manages Brivo-brand hardware only; automated generic hardware control assumption creates device_not_found; automated must have Brivo readers and controllers at each managed door

ACCOUNT HIERARCHY MUST BE UNDERSTOOD: Brivo organizes access as Account → Sites → Groups → Credentials; automated flat permission model creates incorrect access group assignment; automated must model Brivo hierarchy in integration design

SCIM FOR BULK PROVISIONING: Brivo supports SCIM 2.0 for HR-driven user lifecycle management; automated per-user REST API provisioning at enterprise scale creates excessive API calls; automated should use SCIM endpoint for bulk onboarding/offboarding

CREDENTIAL ENROLLMENT IS HARDWARE-DEPENDENT: Physical access cards and mobile credentials require hardware enrollment step; automated fully remote credential provisioning creates unactivated credential; automated must account for physical enrollment workflow
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise access control. SOC 2, GDPR, CCPA. OAuth2. US/EU. Physical access and employee identity data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise access control. SOC 2, GDPR. OAuth2. US/EU. Physical access and employee data.
